name: grep-repo-wide-for-old-behavior-signature-before-deploy
tags: test coverage grep old behavior signature repo wide refactor deploy gate missed test task:15ee2da3
body:
When refactoring away an old behavior (e.g. a specific API response shape, a removed field, a deleted function), grep the whole repo for that behavior's identifying string/signature before considering the refactor done -- not just the unit test co-located with the file you changed. An end-to-end test file (test_hooks_lc.py) asserting the same old response shape (decision=="block") was missed on the first pass because only the directly-adjacent unit test (test_noop_node.py) was checked, causing an avoidable deploy.sh failure on the first attempt.

Why: behavior can be asserted in multiple test layers (unit + integration/end-to-end) that don't share an import path, so fixing the unit test doesn't guarantee the integration test was caught.

How to apply: after removing/changing a behavior, run something like `grep -rn '<old signature>' tests/` (or the equivalent old field/string) across the whole test suite, not just the file adjacent to the code change, before running the full deploy gate.
